title: Estimate Differences
essential_question: How can you use compatible numbers and rounding to estimate differences?

pretest.question: Estimate. 762 − 332 (round to the nearest hundred)
pretest.hint: Round each number to the nearest hundred, then subtract.

expected_errors.note: D: added after rounding (300+300=600) instead of subtracting — wrong_operation

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11 — 'Truncation is a common first attempt at rounding; students need explicit instruction that rounding sometimes requires adding, not just removing.'

expected_errors.note: C: rounded both to hundreds (500−400=100) instead of using compatible numbers

pretest.hint: The word 'about' tells you to estimate. Round to the nearest hundred.

expected_errors.citation: EngageNY G3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — 'Students frequently inspect the wrong digit; explicit labeling of the target place and the deciding digit is required.'

C: about 550 (off; may have not rounded properly)

learn.title: What Does 'Estimate' Mean?
learn.content: When a problem says 'about' or 'estimate,' you don't need the exact answer. You find a number that is close to the exact answer.
learn.math_note: estimate: 어림 — 정확한 계산 없이 근사값을 구하는 것

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.35-38

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

learn.title: Method 1: Compatible Numbers
learn.content: Compatible numbers are numbers that are easy to compute mentally. To estimate 431 − 398: change 431→425 and 398→400. Then 425 − 400 = 25. The estimated difference is about 25.
learn.math_note: compatible numbers: 빼기 쉬운 수로 바꾸어 추정 (431→425, 398→400 → 25)

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.35-38

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

learn.title: Method 2: Rounding to the Nearest Ten
learn.content: Round each number to the nearest ten, then subtract. Example: 762 − 332 → 760 − 330 = 430. The estimated difference is about 430.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.35-38

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

learn.title: Method 2: Rounding to the Nearest Hundred
learn.content: Round each number to the nearest hundred, then subtract. Example: 762 − 332 → 800 − 300 = 500. The estimated difference is about 500.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.35-38

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

learn.title: Which Method Is Better?
learn.content: It depends! Rounding to the nearest ten usually gives a closer estimate. Rounding to the nearest hundred is faster. Compatible numbers can be the most accurate when you choose wisely. Actual: 762 − 332 = 430. Nearest ten: 430. Nearest hundred: 500.
learn.math_note: 두 수가 가까울수록 십의 자리 반올림이 더 정확; 차이가 크면 백의 자리 충분

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.35-38

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

learn.title: When NOT to Round to Hundreds
learn.content: If both numbers round to the same hundred, your estimate would be 0! Example: 436 − 412 → 400 − 400 = 0. That's not useful! Use tens instead: 440 − 410 = 30.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.35-38

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

learn.title: Real-World Example: Yellowfin Tuna
learn.content: A yellowfin tuna weighs 431 pounds. A grouper weighs 398 pounds. About how much heavier is the tuna? Use compatible numbers: 425 − 400 = 25. The tuna is about 25 pounds heavier.
learn.math_note: 실세계: 431파운드 참다랑어 − 398파운드 그루퍼; compat 425−400=25 → 25파운드 더 무거움

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.35-38

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

learn.math_talk_prompt: A yellowfin tuna weighs 431 pounds and a grouper weighs 398 pounds. You estimated the difference using compatible numbers (425 − 400 = 25). Could you have used rounding to the nearest ten instead? Would that give the same estimate? Which method is FASTER for this pair of numbers, and why? What makes a number a 'good' compatible number?

learn.title: Common Mistake Alert!
learn.content: Don't add when you should subtract! If the problem says 'how many more' or 'how much heavier' or 'how many left,' you need to SUBTRACT.
learn.math_note: 흔한 실수: 'how many more/heavier/left' → 빼기(−); 더하기(+) 사용 금지

practice_r2.question: Mia says the estimate for 436 − 412 by rounding to the nearest hundred is 0. Is she correct? Why?
choices: A. Yes, 400−400=0 is a good estimate
B. No, she should round to the nearest ten: 440−410=30
C. No, the answer is exactly 24
D. Yes, both numbers round to 400
practice_r2.hint: If both numbers round to the same hundred, the estimate is 0. That's not helpful!

expected_errors.note: A: agreed 400−400=0 is useful (it is not — produces 0)
C: stated exact answer 24 as the estimate (exact ≠ estimate)
D: also agreed 0 is useful — same error as A

solution_steps: You should use rounding to tens: 520 − 500 = 20

practice_r3.question: There were 418 people at a morning show and 289 at an evening show. Sam estimated the difference as about 200. Maya estimated about 130. Who used rounding to the nearest hundred?
solution_steps: Hmm, that's 100 not 200. Let me reconsider.
Sam's 200 doesn't match either standard method
Sam made a rounding error (289→200 instead of 300)
practice_r3.hint: Calculate what each method gives: hundreds rounding → 400−300=100, tens rounding → 420−290=130.

expected_errors.note: B: chose Maya (correct rounding); but question asks WHO MADE AN ERROR → A
C: said both correct — Maya is correct, Sam is not
D: said neither correct — Maya's 100 IS the correct estimate

practice_r3.note: This is an error-analysis question. Sam rounded 289→200 (incorrect), Maya correctly used tens.
practice_r3.math_note: hundreds: 418→400, 289→300 → 100 (Maya 정답); Sam은 289→200 잘못 반올림 → 200 오답; answer A=Sam이 오류

practice_r3.question: A zookeeper counts 892 visitors on Saturday and 745 on Sunday. She estimates the difference as about 250 using compatible numbers. Which compatible numbers could she have used?
solution_steps: We need compatible numbers close to 892 and 745 that give 250
875 is close to 892, 625 is close to 745? No, 625 is not close to 745
Actually 895−645=250, but that's not a choice
Best answer: 900−650=250 is a plausible compatible pair
practice_r3.hint: Which pair gives exactly 250 as the difference?
practice_r3.note: B is the best answer: 900−650=250 uses compatible subtraction, though 650 is only moderately close to 745.
practice_r3.math_note: 892−745=147 actual; 문제는 '250 추정에 사용한 compatible 쌍'을 묻는다; 875−625=250(C) — 항목 구성이 부정확함(주의)

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.38 SMARTER / Enrichment

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

practice_r3.question: Estimate 703 − 298 three ways and choose the closest to the actual difference.
solution_steps: All methods give 400 for this particular problem
practice_r3.hint: Sometimes all methods give the same answer!

expected_errors.note: A: compatible only (700−300=400) — correct but incomplete; D is better
B: rounding to tens only (700−300=400) — also correct but incomplete
C: duplicate of A (both say 'Compatible: 700−300=400') — item design flaw

practice_r3.note: Corrected: D is the best answer. All methods converge to 400 for this problem.
practice_r3.math_note: 703−298=405 actual; compat 700−300=400, tens 700−300=400, hundreds 700−300=400; 세 방법 모두 400 → answer D [원래 A였으나 D로 수정: 보기 A와 C 중복]

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 8 p.38 SMARTER / Enrichment

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 2 — rounding to nearest 10/100 and compatible-number estimation strategies

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11 — estimation strategies and place-value reasoning for subtraction

practice_r3.question: Jake estimated 547 − 283 as about 300. Emma estimated it as about 260. The actual difference is 264. Whose estimate is closer? Why?
choices: D. Neither is a good estimate
practice_r3.math_note: Jake 500−200=300 off 36; Emma 550−290=260 off 4; actual 264; Emma 더 정확; answer B

practice_r3.question: Math Journal: Which method would you use to estimate 876 − 851 and why?
choices: D. Both B and C give useful estimates
solution_steps: Both B and C give useful estimates
practice_r3.hint: When numbers are close together, hundreds rounding doesn't work well.

expected_errors.note: A: chose hundreds 900−900=0 — produces 0, useless estimate
B: chose tens only — correct but D (both B and C) is more complete
C: chose compatible only — correct but D is more complete

practice_r3.math_note: 876−851=25; hundreds 900−900=0 비유효; tens 880−850=30; compat 875−850=25; B·C 모두 유용; answer D

lesson_summary: 추정은 정확한 계산 없이 합리적인 근사값을 구하는 도구입니다. compatible numbers(빼기 쉬운 수로 바꾸기), 십의 자리 반올림, 백의 자리 반올림 중에서 두 수가 가까울수록 십의 자리 반올림이 더 정확합니다. 어떤 방법을 사용하든 결과가 합리적인지 반드시 확인하세요.
unit_message: 추정은 추측이 아닙니다. 이 단원의 모든 뺄셈 문제에서 '내 답이 합리적인가?' 를 추정으로 먼저 확인하세요.
unit_intro_message: 이 단원에서는 1,000 이내의 덧셈·뺄셈을 배웁니다. 추정(estimation)은 계산 전에 '내 답이 합리적인가?'를 확인하는 핵심 도구입니다.
unit_close_message: 추정은 추측이 아닙니다. 이 단원의 모든 뺄셈 문제에서 compatible numbers나 반올림으로 먼저 추정하고, 정확한 계산이 합리적인지 검증하세요.
